comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: Every American — regardless of their zip code or income — deserves to live in a community that is safe from industrial accidents. Removing or limiting third-party safety audits is a mistake...

I urge the EPA to not rescind major parts of the 2024 "Safer Communities by Chemical Accident Prevention" rule. The new proposal would put American communities at risk by removing requirements for facilities to evaluate natural hazards and limit public data transparency.

The “Common Sense Approach to Chemical Accident Prevention” would roll back nearly all of the protections created by the 2024 Safer Communities Rule. comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: It is crucial that we keep moving forward with chemical accident prevention provisions instead of dialing back, for the sake of our children, grandchildren, and all life.
I do not approve of the changes to risk management proposed in this docket item.

Do not weaken chemical safety requirements which are enacted to prevent disasters that could have substantial health, ecological, and economic impacts. A huge number of Americans - more than half - have homes, schools, places of work close to facilities that store or use dangerous chemicals. These are potential danger zones. Removing the requirement for facilities to be able to identify and properly respond to a release puts both workers and the public at risk.

Instead, I urge you to keep and implement the Safer Communities by Chemical Accident Prevention Rule under the Clean Air Act Risk Management Program and restore public access to chemical hazard information on EPA’s w...

I oppose this proposed regulations change because it will weaken safety rules that protect workers, first responders and fence-line communities from catastrophes like fires and explosions at chemical facilities. More than half of Americans live near a chemical facility. One third of children attend school in a chemically vulnerable zone. Unfortunately, indus...

I'm writing in passionate opposition to the EPA proposal to reduce safeguards regarding chemical emergencies. We live in a dangerous world -- we MUST maintain procedures and funding for the safe response to emergencies. Anything else is short-sighted, irresponsible and reprehensible. Imagine if it were your loved ones affected by the next emergency.

Chemical safety isn’t a political yo-yo. Toxic chemical exposure can cause particular harm to children’s health and during pregnancy, and rolling back chemical safety standards won’t “make America healthy.” Basic protections against industrial chemical fires, explosions, and toxic leaks aren’t something that the Trump administration should keep bouncing back...
We need clean air to live. It is our birthright and necessary for our collective survival.
I oppose this proposal and demand that the EPA continue to allow third-party audits, stop-work orders in hazardous conditions, and public requests for impact reports from regional polluters.

It's horrifying how little this administration cares about our heroes that put their safety and welfare on the line to protect others. Some situations are especially dangerous and hazardous for a responder's health. They should absolutely be protected from the impact of chemical disasters. Corruption should not be allowed to rollback Biden Era protections.

Residents everywhere, all across the country, have the right to safety from toxic chemicals. Companies should, at the barest minimum, be held to the utmost account when it comes to tracking air, water, and soil contaminants, as have been documented byproducts of waste facilities for decades. And at most, these companies should be required to seek and integra...

The Endangerment Finding must stand. The EPA must continue to regulate and oversee greenhouse gas emissions to ensure a healthy and livable planet for current and future generations We urge you to return to the life-saving work of counting – and controlling - ozone and fine particulate matter pollution’s health costs. Our public health depends on it.
